NKJV.1: Praise the LORD!  Praise the LORD, O my soul!
NKJV.2: While I live I will praise the LORD; I will sing praises to my God while I have my being.
NKJV.3: Do not put your trust in princes, Nor in a son of man, in whom there is no help.
NKJV.4: His spirit departs, he returns to his earth; In that very day his plans perish.
NKJV.5: Happy is he who has the God of Jacob for his help, Whose hope is in the LORD his God,
NKJV.6: Who made heaven and earth, The sea, and all that is in them; Who keeps truth forever,
NKJV.7: Who executes justice for the oppressed,  Who gives food to the hungry.  The LORD gives freedom to the prisoners.
NKJV.8: The LORD opens the eyes of the blind; The LORD raises those who are bowed down; The LORD loves the righteous.
NKJV.9: The LORD watches over the strangers; He relieves the fatherless and widow;  But the way of the wicked He turns upside down.
NKJV.10: The LORD shall reign forever— Your God, O Zion, to all generations. Praise the LORD!

KJV.1: Praise<S>1984</S> ye the LORD<S>3050</S>. Praise<S>1984</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>, O my soul<S>5315</S>. <sup>Praise ye: Heb. Hallelujah</sup>
KJV.2: While I live<S>2416</S> will I praise<S>1984</S> the LORD<S>3068</S>: I will sing praises<S>2167</S> unto my God<S>430</S> while I have any being.
KJV.3: Put not your trust<S>982</S> in princes<S>5081</S>, nor in the son<S>1121</S> of man<S>120</S>, in whom there is no help<S>8668</S>. <sup>help: or, salvation</sup>
KJV.4: His breath<S>7307</S> goeth forth<S>3318</S>, he returneth<S>7725</S> to his earth<S>127</S>; in that very day<S>3117</S> his thoughts<S>6250</S> perish<S>6</S>.
KJV.5: Happy<S>835</S> is he that hath the God<S>410</S> of Jacob<S>3290</S> for his help<S>5828</S>, whose hope<S>7664</S> is in the LORD<S>3068</S> his God<S>430</S>:
KJV.6: Which made<S>6213</S> heaven<S>8064</S>, and earth<S>776</S>, the sea<S>3220</S>, and all that therein is: which keepeth<S>8104</S> truth<S>571</S> for ever<S>5769</S>:
KJV.7: Which executeth<S>6213</S> judgment<S>4941</S> for the oppressed<S>6231</S>: which giveth<S>5414</S> food<S>3899</S> to the hungry<S>7457</S>. The LORD<S>3068</S> looseth<S>5425</S> the prisoners<S>631</S>:
KJV.8: The LORD<S>3068</S> openeth<S>6491</S> the eyes of the blind<S>5787</S>: the LORD<S>3068</S> raiseth<S>2210</S> them that are bowed down<S>3721</S>: the LORD<S>3068</S> loveth<S>157</S> the righteous<S>6662</S>:
KJV.9: The LORD<S>3068</S> preserveth<S>8104</S> the strangers<S>1616</S>; he relieveth<S>5749</S> the fatherless<S>3490</S> and widow<S>490</S>: but the way<S>1870</S> of the wicked<S>7563</S> he turneth upside down<S>5791</S>.
KJV.10: The LORD<S>3068</S> shall reign<S>4427</S> for ever<S>5769</S>, even thy God<S>430</S>, O Zion<S>6726</S>, unto all<S>1755</S> generations<S>1755</S>. Praise<S>1984</S> ye the LORD<S>3050</S>.

ESV.1: Praise the LORD! Praise the LORD, O my soul!
ESV.2: I will praise the LORD as long as I live; I will sing praises to my God while I have my being.
ESV.3: Put not your trust in princes, in a son of man, in whom there is no salvation.
ESV.4: When his breath departs, he returns to the earth; on that very day his plans perish.
ESV.5: Blessed is he whose help is the God of Jacob, whose hope is in the LORD his God,
ESV.6: who made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them, who keeps faith forever;
ESV.7: who executes justice for the oppressed, who gives food to the hungry.  The LORD sets the prisoners free;
ESV.8: the LORD opens the eyes of the blind. The LORD lifts up those who are bowed down; the LORD loves the righteous.
ESV.9: The LORD watches over the sojourners; he upholds the widow and the fatherless, but the way of the wicked he brings to ruin.
ESV.10: The LORD will reign forever, your God, O Zion, to all generations. Praise the LORD!

NASB.1: Praise the LORD! Praise the LORD, O my soul!
NASB.2: I will praise the LORD while I live; I will sing praises to my God while I have my being.
NASB.3: Do not trust in princes, In mortal man, in whom there is no salvation.
NASB.4: His spirit departs, he returns to the earth; In that very day his thoughts perish.
NASB.5: How blessed is he whose help is the God of Jacob, Whose hope is in the LORD his God,
NASB.6: Who made heaven and earth, The sea and all that is in them; Who keeps faith forever;
NASB.7: Who executes justice for the oppressed; Who gives food to the hungry. The LORD sets the prisoners free.
NASB.8: The LORD opens [the eyes of] the blind; The LORD raises up those who are bowed down; The LORD loves the righteous;
NASB.9: The LORD protects the strangers; He supports the fatherless and the widow, But He thwarts the way of the wicked.
NASB.10: The LORD will reign forever, Your God, O Zion, to all generations. Praise the LORD!

NLT.1:
Praise the LORD!
Let all that I am praise the LORD.
NLT.2:
I will praise the LORD as long as I live.
I will sing praises to my God with my dying breath.
NLT.3:
Don’t put your confidence in powerful people;
there is no help for you there.
NLT.4:
When they breathe their last, they return to the earth,
and all their plans die with them.
NLT.5:
But joyful are those who have the God of Israel as their helper,
whose hope is in the LORD their God.
NLT.6:
He made heaven and earth,
the sea, and everything in them.
He keeps every promise forever.
NLT.7:
He gives justice to the oppressed
and food to the hungry.
The LORD frees the prisoners.
NLT.8:
The LORD opens the eyes of the blind.
The LORD lifts up those who are weighed down.
The LORD loves the godly.
NLT.9:
The LORD protects the foreigners among us.
He cares for the orphans and widows,
but he frustrates the plans of the wicked.
NLT.10:
The LORD will reign forever.
He will be your God, O Jerusalem, throughout the generations.
Praise the LORD!

WEB.1: Praise Yah! Praise Yahweh, my soul.
WEB.2: While I live, I will praise Yahweh. I will sing praises to my God as long as I exist.
WEB.3: Don’t put your trust in princes, each a son of man in whom there is no help.
WEB.4: His spirit departs, and he returns to the earth. In that very day, his thoughts perish.
WEB.5: Happy is he who has the God of Jacob for his help, whose hope is in Yahweh, his God:
WEB.6: who made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them; who keeps truth forever;
WEB.7: who executes justice for the oppressed; who gives food to the hungry. Yahweh frees the prisoners.
WEB.8: Yahweh opens the eyes of the blind. Yahweh raises up those who are bowed down. Yahweh loves the righteous.
WEB.9: Yahweh preserves the foreigners. He upholds the fatherless and widow, but the way of the wicked he turns upside down.
WEB.10: Yahweh will reign forever; your God, O Zion, to all generations. Praise Yah!

CSB17.2:
I will praise the LORD all my life;
I will sing to my God as long as I live. ⓛ
CSB17.3:
Do not trust in nobles,
in a son of man, [1] who cannot save. ⓜ
CSB17.4:
When his breath [2] leaves him,
he returns to the ground;
on that day his plans die. ⓝ
CSB17.5:
Happy is the one whose help is the God of Jacob,
whose hope is in the LORD his God, ⓐ
CSB17.6:
the Maker of heaven and earth,
the sea and everything in them. ⓑ

CSB17.7:
executing justice for the exploited
and giving food to the hungry. ⓓ
The LORD frees prisoners. ⓔ
CSB17.8:
The LORD opens the eyes of the blind. ⓕ
The LORD raises up those who are oppressed. [3] ⓖ
The LORD loves the righteous. ⓗ
CSB17.9:
The LORD protects resident aliens
and helps the fatherless and the widow, ⓘ

CSB17.10:
The LORD reigns forever;

Hallelujah!

AMP.1:
Praise the LORD! (Hallelujah!)
Praise the LORD, O my soul!
AMP.2:
While I live I will praise the LORD;
I will sing praises to my God as long as I live.
AMP.3:
Do not trust in princes,
In mortal man, in whom there is no salvation (help).
AMP.4:
When his spirit leaves him, he returns to the earth;
In that very day his thoughts and plans perish.
AMP.5:
How blessed and graciously favored is he whose help is the God of Jacob (Israel),
Whose hope is in the LORD his God,
AMP.6:
Who made heaven and earth,
The sea, and all that is in them,
Who keeps truth and is faithful forever,
AMP.7:
Who executes justice for the oppressed,
Who gives food to the hungry.
The LORD sets free the prisoners.
AMP.8:
The LORD opens the eyes of the blind;
The LORD lifts up those who are bowed down;
The LORD loves the righteous [the upright in heart].
AMP.9:
The LORD protects the strangers;
He supports the fatherless and the widow;
But He makes crooked the way of the wicked.
AMP.10:
The LORD shall reign forever,
Your God, O Zion, to all generations.
Praise the LORD! (Hallelujah!)

MSG.2:
All my life long I'll praise God,
singing songs to my God as long as I live.
MSG.3:
Don't put your life in the hands of experts
who know nothing of life, of salvation life.
MSG.4:
Mere humans don't have what it takes;
when they die, their projects die with them.
MSG.5:
Instead, get help from the God of Jacob,
put your hope in God and know real blessing!
MSG.6:
God made sky and soil,
sea and all the fish in it.
He always does what he says—
MSG.7:
he defends the wronged,
he feeds the hungry.
God frees prisoners—
MSG.8:
he gives sight to the blind,
he lifts up the fallen.
God loves good people,
MSG.9:
protects strangers,
takes the side of orphans and widows,
but makes short work of the wicked.
MSG.10:
God's in charge—always.
Zion's God is God for good!
Hallelujah!

NIV.1:
Psalm 146
Praise the Lord.
Praise the Lord, O my soul.
NIV.2:
I will praise the Lord all my life;
I will sing praise to my God as long as I live.
NIV.3:
Do not put your trust in princes,
in mortal men, who cannot save.
NIV.4:
When their spirit departs, they return to the ground;
on that very day their plans come to nothing.
NIV.5:
Blessed is he whose help is the God of Jacob,
whose hope is in the Lord his God,
NIV.6:
the Maker of heaven and earth,
the sea, and everything in them—
the Lord, who remains faithful forever.
NIV.7:
He upholds the cause of the oppressed
and gives food to the hungry.
The Lord sets prisoners free,
NIV.8:
the Lord gives sight to the blind,
the Lord lifts up those who are bowed down,
the Lord loves the righteous.
NIV.9:
The Lord watches over the alien
and sustains the fatherless and the widow,
but he frustrates the ways of the wicked.
NIV.10:
The Lord reigns forever,
your God, O Zion, for all generations.
Praise the Lord.

Praise the LORD!
Praise the LORD, O my soul!
NRSVCE.2:
I will praise the LORD as long as I live;
I will sing praises to my God all my life long.
NRSVCE.3:
Do not put your trust in princes,
in mortals, in whom there is no help.

NET.1: Praise the LORD! Praise the LORD, O my soul!
NET.2: I will praise the LORD as long as I live! I will sing praises to my God as long as I exist!
NET.3: Do not trust in princes, or in human beings, who cannot deliver!
NET.4: Their life’s breath departs, they return to the ground; on that day their plans die.
NET.5: How blessed is the one whose helper is the God of Jacob, whose hope is in the LORD his God,
NET.6: the one who made heaven and earth, the sea, and all that is in them, who remains forever faithful,
NET.7: vindicates the oppressed, and gives food to the hungry. The LORD releases the imprisoned.
NET.8: The LORD gives sight to the blind. The LORD lifts up all who are bent over. The LORD loves the godly.
NET.9: The LORD protects those residing outside their native land; he lifts up the fatherless and the widow, but he opposes the wicked.
NET.10: The LORD rules forever, your God, O Zion, throughout the generations to come! Praise the LORD!
